description_long: This house features a traditional half-timbered facade on the front, with exposed wooden beams forming a crisscross pattern over a light plaster surface. The lower level includes large windows and a main entryway, likely for commercial or residential use, while the upper stories have smaller, symmetrically arranged windows. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in dark shingles, with two prominent chimneys rising above. The sides and rear of the house are plain, with smooth, unadorned plaster walls and minimal window openings, suggesting a focus on the decorative front. The overall structure is tall and narrow, typical of historic European urban architecture. The building exudes an old-world charm, reminiscent of late medieval or early Renaissance townhouses found in cities like Strasbourg or Rothenburg.
description: A tall, narrow house with a decorative half-timbered front, steep gable roof, and plain plastered sides, evoking historic European urban architecture.
description_view_0: The front view showcases the ornate half-timbered facade, symmetrical windows, and the main entryway at street level.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain, unadorned plaster wall with a single small window and two chimneys visible above the roofline.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a flat, featureless plaster wall with one small window and the roof's intersecting angles.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, with a plain plaster wall and no visible windows or decorative elements.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows on front, sparse on sides and rear
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway with a wooden door, set beneath the half-timbered facade, no porch or steps visible, framed by large ground-floor windows.
